title: Running ubi9 on Apple silicon breaks on unsupported x86_64-v2 instruction set
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I'm trying to build and run an AMD64 ubi9 image on Apple silicon. I have installed qemu-user-binfmt in the Fedora CoreOS VM, but when I start a podman build with a Containerfile that starts with `FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest`, the build fails with 

```
Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2
```

1. On Apple silicon, brew install podman, set up a VM

2. Try and run a build with a Containerfile that has a `FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest` and with `--arch amd64`

3. See error

**Describe the results you received:**
```
✗ podman build --arch amd64 -f Containerfile -t testing:0.1 .
STEP 1/7: FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob sha256:743b2fff6512b752ffd624ae8b45a951ca8378f3d84a912061cdaf508db1a29d
Copying blob sha256:961425e673a750fb2d17875c2a4bf5a319c8681e4fdf81a5a6e2d819cef95e35
Copying config sha256:4a8128b051b8bfafcc6bd540db324a5244aff1ef7bd9f6dda7ecd10680c4fbbe
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/7: RUN microdnf install -y python3 python3-pip
Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2
Error: error building at STEP "RUN microdnf install -y python3 python3-pip": error while running runtime: exit status 127
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful build

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.5

```

comments.body:
The error `Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2` is very telling, as that indicates that the architecture level of the x86_64 emulation does not expose instructions in the level v2, such as `sse`.

Inside the VM `qemu-user-static` is used to run an x86_64 container after the command:

```
$ podman run --arch x86_64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal
```

is run. This fails now and this can be confirmed with the call:

```
$ qemu-x86_64-static ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 --help
```

as most likely this returns something like:

```
  x86-64-v4
  x86-64-v3
  x86-64-v2
```

instead of:

```
  x86-64-v4
  x86-64-v3
  x86-64-v2 (supported, searched)
```

which means the CPU does not have the needed compatibility. Since I do not have access to an M1 for the moment I have asked @cfergeau and @praveenkumar to have a look at this. We most likely run into the same issue with vfkit and crc.

So I'm probably daft, but I'm not really sure where to find that ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 binary? If I take the one I have in one of the overlay directories from an ubi8 image, I indeed get this:

```
his program interpreter self-identifies as: /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2

Shared library search path:
  (libraries located via /etc/ld.so.cache)
  /lib64 (system search path)
  /usr/lib64 (system search path)

Subdirectories of glibc-hwcaps directories, in priority order:
  x86-64-v4
  x86-64-v3
  x86-64-v2

Legacy HWCAP subdirectories under library search path directories:
  i686 (AT_PLATFORM; supported, searched)
  tls (supported, searched)
  avx512_1
  x86_64 (supported, searched)
```

Right, this file is only inside the x86_64 ubi image (either run from podman or with qemu-user-static). This means the requested support is not there.

Since you were able to get this output, what is the actual `cat /proc/cpuinfo` from that same environment?

```
processor	: 0
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0
```

Seeing `CPU architecture: 8` most likely means that ths is the output from the podman VM directly, as that might indicate `Aarch64`. 

Inside the podman VM try to do the following
```
$ podman run --arch amd64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal cat /proc/cpuinfo
```

@wzzrd / @maxim-rapidapi colleague just now indicated that the image also fails to do `cat` with the same issue.. perhaps ubi8 as you tried might.

---


```
$ podman run --arch amd64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-minimal cat /proc/cpuinfo
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob sha256:67d8ef4787324d6b980e182d889faf591c9bce6aabf0ca370cf29bb0843962e4
Copying blob sha256:a96e4e55e78a64a3e6b57d5e6824ee4f3550d2027553ed26eaf30763894fb606
Copying blob sha256:67d8ef4787324d6b980e182d889faf591c9bce6aabf0ca370cf29bb0843962e4
Copying blob sha256:a96e4e55e78a64a3e6b57d5e6824ee4f3550d2027553ed26eaf30763894fb606
Copying config sha256:c9882b8114e3f3116587da63c060cbf877b62f4e0f5dda584b0edbf14517dd44
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
processor	: 0
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0

processor	: 1
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0
```


so perhaps this might not provide the needed info. as it looks pretty much what the VM directly would return. Let me ponder about this... 


---

```
$ ssh crc -- cat /proc/cpuinfo
Warning: Permanently added '[127.0.0.1]:2222' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
no such identity: /Users/prkumar/.crc/machines/crc/id_rsa: No such file or directory
processor	: 0
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0

processor	: 1
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0
```

So yes, as expected... it is the same output as what the VM would return itself, not the emulated CPU.

---

Even `lscpu` is not useful as it uses the same output of `cpuinfo` as can be seen in the architecture and flags.

```
$ podman run --arch amd64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8 lscpu 
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Architecture:        x86_64
CPU op-mode(s):      32-bit
Byte Order:          Little Endian
CPU(s):              2
On-line CPU(s) list: 0,1
Thread(s) per core:  1
Core(s) per socket:  2
Socket(s):           1
NUMA node(s):        1
Vendor ID:           0x00
Model:               0
Stepping:            0x0
BogoMIPS:            48.00
NUMA node0 CPU(s):   0,1
Flags:               fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
```

It's a limitation in qemu-user-static-x86 binfmt support. rhel9 expects a CPU supporting the x86_64-v2 instruction set, but qemu-user-static binfmt support is configured to run `qemu-x86_64-static` directly (see `/usr/lib/binfmt.d/qemu-x86_64-static.conf`

ssh'ing into a linux VM running on the M1 and running `ld-linux-x86_64.so.2 --help` returns:
```
$ qemu-x86_64-static ./ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 --help |grep x86-64
Usage: ./ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 [OPTION]... EXECUTABLE-FILE [ARGS-FOR-PROGRAM...]
This program interpreter self-identifies as: /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2
  x86-64-v4
  x86-64-v3
  x86-64-v2
```

It's possible to use `--cpu` as an argument to `qemu-x86_64-static` to get something better:
```
$ qemu-x86_64-static --cpu Haswell-v2  ./ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 --help |grep x86-64
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.fma [bit 12]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.pcid [bit 17]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.x2apic [bit 21]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.tsc-deadline [bit 24]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.avx [bit 28]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.01H:ECX.f16c [bit 29]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.07H:EBX.avx2 [bit 5]
qemu-x86_64-static: warning: TCG doesn't support requested feature: CPUID.07H:EBX.invpcid [bit 10]
Usage: ./ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 [OPTION]... EXECUTABLE-FILE [ARGS-FOR-PROGRAM...]
This program interpreter self-identifies as: /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2
  x86-64-v4
  x86-64-v3
  x86-64-v2 (supported, searched)
```
(the important bit is the last line)

I tried to configure binfmt to use an intermediate bash script or go program to start qemu-x86_64-static with the correct arguments, this worked when trying to start an x86_64 binary directly from the command line inside the VM, but when I then try to use `podman run`, this fails:
```
$ podman run  --arch x86_64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal /usr/bin/ls
2022/08/24 15:55:48 no such file or directory
```
(with an intermediate go binary). With a shell script, this gives an error message about too many symlinks. I'm not quite sure what's wrong/what's confusing podman.
This needs further investigation for sure. I have put this on our tech sync schedule/agenda.

Build the binary (shell gives symlink error indeed) but I get

```
podman run -it --rm --arch amd64 registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:latest /bin/sh
2022/08/24 18:57:21 no such file or directory
```

Definitely progress though :)
The issue is about how to get this correctly configured to allow `binfmt_misc` and `podman` to correctly run the binaries with the changed `-cpu` setting.

Responding to question in Twitter whether this problem also occurs with Docker instead of Podman:

```sh
docker run --platform linux/amd64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal cat /proc/cpuinfo
Unable to find image 'registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest' locally
latest: Pulling from ubi9-minimal
961425e673a7: Pull complete
743b2fff6512: Pull complete
Digest: sha256:4ba4d3e2da3a7edb8a8e79fd38e95702f445aa4d5a66a1b1667260b5f1405acc
Status: Downloaded newer image for registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal:latest
processor	: 0
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0

processor	: 1
BogoMIPS	: 48.00
Features	: fp asimd evtstrm aes pmull sha1 sha2 crc32 atomics fphp asimdhp cpuid asimdrdm jscvt fcma lrcpc dcpop sha3 asimddp sha512 asimdfhm dit uscat ilrcpc flagm ssbs sb paca pacg dcpodp flagm2 frint
CPU implementer	: 0x00
CPU architecture: 8
CPU variant	: 0x0
CPU part	: 0x000
CPU revision	: 0
```
In short, since `cat` from the ubi9 container image runs on Docker I can conclude that it uses the `-v2` architecture, while the podman VM doesn't

docker desktop is using `/containers/services/binfmt/rootfs/usr/bin/qemu-x86_64` to run x86_64 binaries on ARM, and by default their build of qemu-x86_64 has x86_64-v2 support (tested by running `ld-linux-x86_64.so.2 --help` with it)

this means they are using a modified/patched binary. It might be good to pick this up with @dustymabe and Fedora too.

The original request was handled here, but this was only the break up of the packages: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1088

> this means they are using a modified/patched binary.

Most likely https://github.com/tonistiigi/binfmt/blob/master/patches/cpu-max/0001-default-to-cpu-max-on-x86-and-arm.patch

> this means they are using a modified/patched binary. It might be good to pick this up with @dustymabe and Fedora too.
> 
> The original request was handled here, but this was only the break up of the packages: [coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#1088](https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1088)

We just install the `qemu-user-static-x86` package. Any issues would need to be taken up with `qemu` (link to bugzilla can be found here: https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/qemu )

@cfergeau perhaps good to confirm which targets are affected. I would expect:

  * Fedora, FCOS
  * RHEL, RHCOS
  * CentOS, CentOS Stream
Hi @crobinso, perhaps you have an idea how best to fix this?

I have written a quick and dirty program to test the feature level of an x86 CPU here (it will also list which features of a feature level is supported and which are missing): https://github.com/CKingX/x86-feature-test/releases/tag/v1.0.0 This may be of interest to this issue.

> @cfergeau perhaps good to confirm which targets are affected. I would expect:
> 
>     * Fedora, FCOS
> 
>     * RHEL, RHCOS
> 
>     * CentOS, CentOS Stream

Fedora 36/rawhide are working fine, CentOS Stream 9 and RHEL9 require x86_64-v2 support:
```
# podman run -it --rm --arch x86_64 registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:36 echo "hello world"
hello world

# podman run -it --rm --arch x86_64 registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9 echo "hello world"
Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2

# podman run -it --rm --arch x86_64 quay.io/centos/centos:stream9 echo "hello world"
Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2
```

I got more details regarding how podman/binfmt work together. podman isn't doing anything special with binfmt. When starting a container, it enters a mount namespace corresponding to the filesystem of the container image, and then it's exec'ing /bin/bash or whatever the user asks it to run in the container. If it's a native binary, all is fine, if it's a non-native binary (x86_64 on aarch64 for example), then the kernel binfmt support kicks in. 
qemu-user uses the 'F' flag in its binfmt configuration (see https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/v5.19/admin-guide/binfmt-misc.html ), which means `/usr/bin/qemu-x86_64-static` will be found regardless of the currently active mount namespace/changeroot/...   However, this binary will be running in the mount namespace context, which means it will only be able to access the files available in the container image. This in particular means that we can't use a helper process which will then invoke qemu-x86_64-static with the right flags, as the latter is not going to be present in the container image filesystem.

https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/v5.19/admin-guide/binfmt-misc.html is also quite clear that it's not possible to pass additional arguments to the binfmt interpreter:
> If you want to pass special arguments to your interpreter, you can write a wrapper script for it. 

At this point, the best course of action will be to try to change the default CPU used by qemu-user-static-x86 so that it has x86_64-v2 support.

> Fedora 36/rawhide are working fine, 

I mean as a host, running UBI9 images

> `# podman run -it --rm --arch x86_64 quay.io/centos/centos:stream9 echo "hello world"`
> `Fatal glibc error: CPU does not support x86-64-v2`

This was expected, but good to know nonetheless. From this we can conclude the CentOS9 stream as a host will act the same as RHEL9, and therefore most likely not run `-v2` by default for userspace emulation

> I mean as a host, running UBI9 images

Fedora 36 (FCOS36) is what we've been testing so far as this is what podman-machine/crc podman support are based on. RHEL9 does not have qemu-user-static packages, so it's not going to work. I haven't looked at centos9.

> RHEL9 does not have qemu-user-static 

ugh, right... plus they are also not in EPEL

After [rebuilding Fedora36 QEMU](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/taskinfo?taskID=91274538) with a different default for qemu-user's CPU parameter and replacing the `qemu-x86_64-static` binary in my VM:
```
$  podman run  --arch x86_64 -it --rm registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9-minimal /usr/bin/ls
afs  bin  boot	dev  etc  home	lib  lib64  lost+found	media  mnt  opt  proc  root  run  sbin	srv  sys  tmp  usr  var
```

The patch I added to the RPM is:
```
From 25912b39cf4159bee69445e19bf0a2d8155df962 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: rpm-build <rpm-build>
Date: Thu, 25 Aug 2022 17:04:28 +0200
Subject: [PATCH] linux-user: Change default cpu model for x86_64

qemu64 does not support x86_64-v2, which is needed to run f36/el9
binaries.
---
 linux-user/x86_64/target_elf.h | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/linux-user/x86_64/target_elf.h b/linux-user/x86_64/target_elf.h
index 7b76a90..8f11b56 100644
--- a/linux-user/x86_64/target_elf.h
```

Issue filed against Fedora QEMU package at https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2121700
FYI, Daniel Berrangé sent an email to the Qemu mailinglist around this: https://lists.gnu.org/archive/html/qemu-devel/2022-08/msg04066.html
I'm not 100% sure, but I don't think the proposed patch was ever merged, was it? Maybe it was combined with another change or renamed?
Should podman address this issue by introducing the new `--arch` architecture?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This does not seem to be a Podman issue, closing, keep the conversation going here. Reopen if I am mistaken.

This is fixed in [qemu-7.0.0-13.fc37](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/search?terms=qemu-7.0.0-13.fc37&type=build&match=exact) and [qemu-6.2.0-17.fc36](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/search?terms=qemu-6.2.0-17.fc36&type=build&match=exact), when these packages reach the aarch64 images from https://getfedora.org/coreos this bug will finally be fixed

If someone wants to make a request at https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues we'll try to have it in the next round of `testing`/`next` releases (and then `stable` the cycle after that).
fedora-coreos includes qemu?
